---
title: "Uncovered Interest Rate Parity (UIRP) Analysis"
editor: source
format:
  html: 
    page-layout: article
    # html-table-processing: none
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show code"
    df-print: kable
    code-block-border-left: "royalblue"
    code-block-bg: true
    toc: true
    self-contained: true
    embed-resources: true

  # typst:
  #   latex-engine: xelatex
  #   embed-resources: true
  #   code-block: false
  #   citation-package: natbib
  #   includes:
  #     in-header: header.tex
  #   code-tools: true
  #   code-fold: true
  #   df-print: kable
  #   number-sections: true
toc-depth: 2
fig-align: center
number-sections: true
lang: en
# author:
#   - name: Pietro Rota
#     affiliation:
#       - name: Bayes Business School – City, St George’s University of London
#         city: London
#         state: UK
#         url: https://www.bayes.citystgeorges.ac.uk/
---

| Name              | Student Id | E-Mail Address                         |
| ----------------- | ---------- | -------------------------------------- |
| Delalande, Paul   | 220008469  | <Paul.Delalande@citystgeorges.ac.uk>   |
| Martinsson, Alvin | 250023511  | <Alvin.Martinsson@citystgeorges.ac.uk> |
| Porter, James     | 250057482  | <James.Porter@citystgeorges.ac.uk>     |
| Rota, Pietro      | 250052269  | <Pietro.Rota@citystgeorges.ac.uk>      |



# Introduction
Currency Pair: JPY/USD | Frequency: Daily | Rates: 12-Month Money Market

```{python}
#| label: import
from great_tables import GT
import selenium
import playwright
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.gridspec as gridspec
from matplotlib.ticker import AutoMinorLocator
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")
import statsmodels.api as sm
from scipy import stats

plt.rcParams["figure.constrained_layout.use"] = True
# violin plot 
import ptitprince as pt
import seaborn as sns
```

## Load Data and Variable Creation

```{python}
#| label: data
#| include: false
FILE_PATH = "C:\\Users\\pietr\\OneDrive - City St George's, University of London\\Documents\\a2025-2026 BAYES\\IF - International Finance\\IF - Course Work\\DATA UIP OFFLINE.xlsx"

SJU = pd.read_excel(FILE_PATH, sheet_name = "USDJPY",  skiprows=5)
IU  = pd.read_excel(FILE_PATH, sheet_name = "USD 12M", skiprows=5)
IJ  = pd.read_excel(FILE_PATH, sheet_name = "JPY 12M", skiprows=5)

sju_clean = SJU[['Date', 'PX_LAST']].rename(columns={'PX_LAST': 'Spot_Rate'})
iu_clean  = IU[['Date', 'PX_LAST']].rename(columns={'PX_LAST': 'USD_12M'})
ij_clean  = IJ[['Date', 'PX_LAST']].rename(columns={'PX_LAST': 'JPY_12M'})

# 3. Merge them step-by-step
df = pd.merge(sju_clean, iu_clean, on='Date', how='left')
df = pd.merge(df, ij_clean, on='Date', how='left')

df.columns = ["Date", "Spot", "IR_US", "IR_JP"]
df = df.dropna().sort_values("Date").reset_index(drop=True)
```

```{python}
divider = 1 / 12 / 100
df["IR_JP"] = df["IR_JP"] * divider
df["IR_US"] = df["IR_US"] * divider

main_y = "dS"

if main_y == "dS" : 
    print(f"This regression is using {main_y} which means Log change") 
elif main_y == "dSp":
    print(f"This regression is using {main_y} which means Percentage change") 
else : 
    print("The file will yield an error")
```

Note on the interest rate differential we are using  Japan minus US not because we are representing japan but because thats how our exchange rate is structured in how many yen does it take to get a dollar

```{python}
#| label: var_creation
# Log change in exchange rate (JPY/USD): positive = JPY depreciation
df["dS"] = np.log(df["Spot"]).diff()              # in log
df["dSp"] = df["Spot"]/df["Spot"].shift(1)-1      # in %  

# Interest rate differential: Japan minus US (domestic - foreign)
df["Diff"] = (df["IR_JP"] - df["IR_US"])
df["Diff_lag"] = df["Diff"].shift(1)


df_plot = df.dropna().copy()

T = len(df)
print(f"Sample: {df.iloc[0]["Date"].strftime("%Y-%m-%d")} -> {df.iloc[len(df)-1]["Date"].strftime("%Y-%m-%d")}  |  T = {T}\n")
df_plot
```

# DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS

```{python}
#| html-table-processing: none
# 1. Update labels to be explicit about units and frequency
desc_vars = {
    "Spot Rate (JPY/USD)"         : df_plot["Spot"],
    "Log ΔSpot (Daily)"           : df_plot["dS"],
    "Japan IR (Monthly equiv.)"   : df_plot["IR_JP"],
    "US IR (Monthly equiv.)"      : df_plot["IR_US"],
    "Interest Rate Diff (JP-US)"  : df_plot["Diff"],
}

stats_rows = []
for label, series in desc_vars.items():
    stats_rows.append({
        "Series"    : label,
        "Obs"       : int(series.count()),
        "Mean"      : series.mean(),
        "Std Dev"   : series.std(),
        "Min"       : series.min(),
        "25%"       : series.quantile(0.25),
        "Median"    : series.median(),
        "75%"       : series.quantile(0.75),
        "Max"       : series.max(),
        "Skewness"  : series.skew(),
        "Kurtosis"  : series.kurt(),
        
    })

stats_df = pd.DataFrame(stats_rows).set_index("Series")


stats_df.columns = ['Count', 'Mean', 'Std', 'Min', '25%', '50%', '75%', 'Max', 'Skew', 'Kurt']
stats_df["Name"] = stats_df.index

# 2. Refined GT Formatting
GTTTdes2 = (
    GT(stats_df)
    .cols_move_to_start(columns="Name")
    .tab_header(
        title="Descriptive Statistics",
        subtitle="Sample Period: March 2010 – March 2026"
    )
    .opt_stylize(5, color = "gray")
    .fmt_integer(columns = "Count")
    # Format Rates and Changes as Percentages
    .fmt(lambda x: f"{x:.3%}", 
         rows=[1,2,3,4], 
         columns=['Mean', 'Std', 'Min', '25%', '50%', '75%', 'Max'])
    # Format Spot Rate, Skew, and Kurtosis as standard floats (NOT percentages)
    .fmt(lambda x: f"{x:.2f}", 
         rows=[0], 
         columns=['Mean', 'Std', 'Min', '25%', '50%', '75%', 'Max'])
    .fmt(lambda x: f"{x:.2f}", 
         columns=["Skew", "Kurt"]) 
    .tab_source_note(source_note="Note: Interest rates are expressed as monthly equivalents (Annual Rate / 12).")
)
GTTTdes2
```

# TIMELINE PLOTS

```{python}
#| label: plot1
#| layout-ncol: 1

STYLE = {'spot': {'color': '#1a1a2e', 'lw': 1.0},
 'ds': {'color': '#e94560', 'lw': 0.8},
 'ir_jp': {'color': '#0f3460', 'lw': 1.2},
 'ir_us': {'color': '#e94560', 'lw': 1.2},
 'diff': {'color': '#533483', 'lw': 1.2},
 'zero': {'color': 'black', 'lw': 0.6, 'ls': '--', 'alpha': 0.4}}

def style_ax(ax, title, ylabel, xlabel="Date"):
    ax.set_facecolor("#ffffff")
    ax.set_title(title, fontsize=14, fontweight="bold", pad=6, loc="left")
    ax.set_ylabel(ylabel, fontsize=9)
    ax.set_xlabel(xlabel, fontsize=9)
    ax.tick_params(axis="both", labelsize=8)
    ax.xaxis.set_minor_locator(AutoMinorLocator())
    ax.grid(which="major", color="#e0e0e0", lw=0.6)
    ax.grid(which="minor", color="#f0f0f0", lw=0.3)
    ax.spines[["top", "right"]].set_visible(False)

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 4))
ax = plt.gca()
ax.plot(df_plot["Date"], df_plot["Spot"], **STYLE["spot"])
style_ax(ax, title = "JPY/USD Spot Exchange Rate", ylabel = "JPY per USD")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 4))
ax = plt.gca()
ax.plot(df_plot["Date"], df_plot["IR_JP"]*100, label="Japan 12M", **STYLE["ir_jp"])
ax.plot(df_plot["Date"], df_plot["IR_US"]*100, label="US 12M", **STYLE["ir_us"])
ax.legend(fontsize=8, framealpha=0.8, loc="upper left")
style_ax(ax, "12-Month Interest Rates (%)", ylabel = "Rate (%)")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 4))
ax = plt.gca()
ax.plot(df_plot["Date"], df_plot["Diff"]*100, **STYLE["diff"])
ax.axhline(0, **STYLE["zero"])
ax.fill_between(df_plot["Date"], df_plot["Diff"]*100, 0, where=df_plot["Diff"] >= 0, alpha=0.2, color="#0f3460")
ax.fill_between(df_plot["Date"], df_plot["Diff"]*100, 0, where=df_plot["Diff"] < 0,  alpha=0.2, color="#e94560")
style_ax(ax, "Interest Rate Differential: Japan − US (%)", ylabel="Differential (%)")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

```

# OLS

UIP REGRESSION: 

$$
dS_t = \alpha + \beta*(i_{JP} - i_{US})_{t-1} + \varepsilon _t
$$

```{python}
#| label: ols
reg = df[[main_y, "Diff_lag"]].dropna().copy()

y = reg[main_y]
X = sm.add_constant(reg["Diff_lag"])

ols = sm.OLS(y, X).fit()

ols.summary()
```


```{python}
#| label: NW tests
NW_LAGS = 10   # Newey-West lags (rule of thumb: 4*(T/100)^(2/9) ≈ 10 for T~4000)
nw  = ols.get_robustcov_results(cov_type="HAC", maxlags=NW_LAGS, use_correction=True)

alpha_hat = ols.params["const"]
beta_hat  = ols.params["Diff_lag"]
T_reg     = int(ols.nobs)

def t_test(estimate, se, h0_value, label):
    t_stat = (estimate - h0_value) / se
    p_val  = 2 * stats.t.sf(abs(t_stat), df=T_reg - 2)
    return {"Hypothesis": label, "Estimate": round(estimate, 6),
            "H0 Value": h0_value, "Std Error": round(se, 6),
            "t-stat": round(t_stat, 4), "p-value": round(p_val, 4),
            "Reject H0 (5%)?": "YES" if p_val < 0.05 else "NO"}

# Standard OLS s.e.
ols_se_a = ols.bse[0]
ols_se_b = ols.bse[1]

# Newey-West s.e.
nw_se_a  = nw.bse[0]
nw_se_b  = nw.bse[1]

tests_ols = [
    t_test(alpha_hat, ols_se_a, 0, "H0: alpha = 0  (OLS SE)"),
    t_test(beta_hat,  ols_se_b, 1, "H0: beta  = 1  (OLS SE)"),
]
tests_nw = [
    t_test(alpha_hat, nw_se_a, 0, "H0: alpha = 0  (NW SE)"),
    t_test(beta_hat,  nw_se_b, 1, "H0: beta  = 1  (NW SE)"),
]

# Joint Wald test: alpha=0 AND beta=1 simultaneously (Newey-West)
R = np.array([[1, 0],
              [0, 1]])
r = np.array([0, 1])

# wald_nw = nw.wald_test(sm.stats.contrast.ContrastResults, np.eye(2))
# Manual Wald using NW covariance
theta = np.array([alpha_hat, beta_hat])
r_vec = np.array([0, 1])
R_mat = np.eye(2)
diff  = R_mat @ theta - r_vec
V     = nw.cov_params()
W     = diff @ np.linalg.inv(R_mat @ V @ R_mat.T) @ diff
wald_p = 1 - stats.chi2.cdf(W, df=2)

print(f"  T = {T_reg}  |  Newey-West lags = {NW_LAGS}")
print(f"\n  alpha (intercept) = {alpha_hat:.6f}")
print(f"  beta  (slope)     = {beta_hat:.6f}")
print(f"  R-squared         = {ols.rsquared:.6f}")
print(f"  Adj. R-squared    = {ols.rsquared_adj:.6f}")


print("\n--- Individual t-tests ---")
for t in tests_ols + tests_nw:
    print(f"  {t['Hypothesis']:<38}  t={t['t-stat']:>8.4f}  p={t['p-value']:.4f}  "
          f"Reject? {t['Reject H0 (5%)?']}")

print(f"\n--- Joint Wald test: alpha=0 AND beta=1 (Newey-West) ---")
print(f"  Chi2(2) = {W:.4f}   p-value = {wald_p:.4f}   "
      f"Reject H0 (5%)? {'YES' if wald_p < 0.05 else 'NO'}")
```

## Rolling regression
Performing a rolling regression is a tool to see how the $\beta$ coefficient of the UIP equation evolves. Over 15 years, you are likely to see it swing between negative (the Fama Puzzle) and positive (the "New Fama" or fundamental regime).
```{python}
#| label: rolling daily
#| layout-ncol: 1
from statsmodels.regression.rolling import RollingOLS
y = reg[main_y]
X = sm.add_constant(reg['Diff_lag']*100)

# Define the window size 
window_size = 180  # 1/2 year of daily data 

# Run the rolling regression
model = RollingOLS(y, X, window=window_size)
rolling_res = model.fit()
rolling_r2 = rolling_res.rsquared

# 4. Extract the coefficients (beta) and the 95% (1.96 is alpha/2) confidence intervals
params = rolling_res.params['Diff_lag']
std_err = rolling_res.bse['Diff_lag']
upper_ci = params + (1.96 * std_err)
lower_ci = params - (1.96 * std_err)

# 5. Plotting
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
plt.plot(df["Date"].drop(1), params, label='Rolling Beta (UIP Coefficient)', color='blue')
# plt.plot(rolling_r2, label='Rolling R^2', color='black')
plt.fill_between(df["Date"].drop(1), lower_ci, upper_ci, color='blue', alpha=0.1, label='95% CI')
plt.axhline(y=1, color='red', linestyle='--', label='Theoretical UIP (Beta=1)')
plt.axhline(y=0, color='black', linewidth=0.8)
plt.title(f'Rolling UIP Regression ({window_size}-Period Window)')
plt.legend()

import ruptures as rpt


points = params.dropna().values
df["rollBETA"] = np.concat([[np.nan] *(window_size), points])
# 2. Use the "Pelt" algorithm to find breaks in the mean of the Beta
# 'model="l2"' looks for shifts in the average value of the beta
algo = rpt.Pelt(model="l2").fit(points)
result = algo.predict(pen=10) # 'pen' controls how 'strict' it is about finding a break

# You can then map these indices back to your dataframe dates.
break_dates = params.dropna().index[result[:-1]]

for i, b_date in enumerate(break_dates):
    plt.axvline(x=df[df.index == b_date]["Date"], color='darkorange', linestyle=':', lw=2, label='Structural Break' if i == 0 else "")
plt.grid(alpha=0.3)
plt.legend()
plt.show()

df[df.index.isin(break_dates)]
```

**Possible explanation for turnoil in 2012-2014**

In 2012, US-Japan relations faced instability driven by Japan's domestic political turbulence, economic recession, and contentious base realignment issues in Okinawa. Japan’s rapid leadership turnover and high debt created uncertainty, while disputes over the Futenma air station relocation strained security cooperation amidst rising regional tensions with China. 

In 2013, US-Japan relations faced instability largely driven by rising maritime tensions between Japan and China over the Senkaku Islands, causing concern that the US-Japan security alliance could be drawn into a conflict. Prime Minister Shinzo Abe sought stronger assurances of US defense, but tensions were exacerbated by regional disputes, economic concerns, and anxieties over the reliability of US protection. 

In 2014, U.S.-Japan relations were marked by tension over trade negotiations, specifically the Trans-Pacific Partnership (TPP) and agricultural tariffs. Other factors included Japan's security challenges with China, disagreements over hosting U.S. troops, and Japan's push for a "Proactive Contribution to Peace"

## Does UIP work better for daily data turned into monthly?

```{python}
#| label: monthly
df_monthly = df.resample('ME', on='Date')[['Spot', 'IR_JP', 'IR_US']].last().reset_index()
df_monthly["Diff"] = df_monthly["IR_JP"] - df_monthly["IR_US"]
df_monthly["Diff_lag"] = df_monthly["Diff"].shift(1)
df_monthly["dS"] = np.log(df_monthly["Spot"]).diff()                      # in log
df_monthly["dSp"] = df_monthly["Spot"]/df_monthly["Spot"].shift(1)-1      # in %  
reg = df_monthly[[main_y, "Diff_lag"]].dropna().copy()

y = reg[main_y]
X = sm.add_constant(reg["Diff_lag"])

ols = sm.OLS(y, X).fit()

ols.summary()
```

```{python}
#| label: monthly tests
#| include: false
NW_LAGS = 10   # Newey-West lags (rule of thumb: 4*(T/100)^(2/9) ≈ 10 for T~4000)
nw  = ols.get_robustcov_results(cov_type="HAC", maxlags=NW_LAGS, use_correction=True)

alpha_hat = ols.params["const"]
beta_hat  = ols.params["Diff_lag"]
T_reg     = int(ols.nobs)

# Standard OLS s.e.
ols_se_a = ols.bse[0]
ols_se_b = ols.bse[1]

# Newey-West s.e.
nw_se_a  = nw.bse[0]
nw_se_b  = nw.bse[1]

tests_ols = [
    t_test(alpha_hat, ols_se_a, 0, "H0: alpha = 0  (OLS SE)"),
    t_test(beta_hat,  ols_se_b, 1, "H0: beta  = 1  (OLS SE)"),
]
tests_nw = [
    t_test(alpha_hat, nw_se_a, 0, "H0: alpha = 0  (NW SE)"),
    t_test(beta_hat,  nw_se_b, 1, "H0: beta  = 1  (NW SE)"),
]

# Joint Wald test: alpha=0 AND beta=1 simultaneously (Newey-West)
R = np.array([[1, 0],
              [0, 1]])
r = np.array([0, 1])

# wald_nw = nw.wald_test(sm.stats.contrast.ContrastResults, np.eye(2))
# Manual Wald using NW covariance
theta = np.array([alpha_hat, beta_hat])
r_vec = np.array([0, 1])
R_mat = np.eye(2)
diff  = R_mat @ theta - r_vec
V     = nw.cov_params()
W     = diff @ np.linalg.inv(R_mat @ V @ R_mat.T) @ diff
wald_p = 1 - stats.chi2.cdf(W, df=2)

print(f"  T = {T_reg}  |  Newey-West lags = {NW_LAGS}")
print(f"\n  alpha (intercept) = {alpha_hat:.6f}")
print(f"  beta  (slope)     = {beta_hat:.6f}")
print(f"  R-squared         = {ols.rsquared:.6f}")
print(f"  Adj. R-squared    = {ols.rsquared_adj:.6f}")


print("\n--- Individual t-tests ---")
for t in tests_ols + tests_nw:
    print(f"  {t['Hypothesis']:<38}  t={t['t-stat']:>8.4f}  p={t['p-value']:.4f}  "
          f"Reject? {t['Reject H0 (5%)?']}")

print(f"\n--- Joint Wald test: alpha=0 AND beta=1 (Newey-West) ---")
print(f"  Chi2(2) = {W:.4f}   p-value = {wald_p:.4f}   "
      f"Reject H0 (5%)? {'YES' if wald_p < 0.05 else 'NO'}")
```

```{python}
#| label: bigtable
# Create the data
#| html-table-processing: none

data = {
    "Parameter": ["α (Constant)", "β (IR Diff, lagged)"],
    "Estimate": [0.000179, 0.018355],
    "Std Error (NW)": [0.000121, 0.000663],
    "t-stat (H₀: α=0 β=1)": [1.48, -14.82],
    "p-value": ["0.174", "< 0.001"]
}

cooldata = pd.DataFrame(data)

# Create GT table
GTTTtest1 = (
    GT(cooldata)
    .tab_header(title="UIP Regression Results")
    .opt_stylize(5, "gray")
)

GTTTtest1
```

```{python}
#| label: bigtable2
#| html-table-processing: none

# Create the table data
data = {
    "Hypothesis": [
        "H₀: α = 0",
        "H₀: β = 1 (NW SE)",
        "H₀: α=0 & β=1 (Wald)",
        "Monthly: H₀: β = 1 (NW SE)",
        "Monthly: H₀: α=0 & β=1 (Wald)"
    ],
    "Test Statistic": [
        f"t = 1.48",
        f"t = -14.82",
        f"χ² = 438.97",
        f"t = -0.514",
        f"t = 6.008"
    ],
    "p-value": [
        f"0.174",
        f"< 0.001",
        f"< 0.001",
        f"0.608",
        f"0.042"
    ],
    "Decision": [
        "Fail to Reject",
        "Strongly Rejected",
        "Strongly Rejected",
        "Fail to Reject",
        "Rejected"
    ]
}

cooldata = pd.DataFrame(data)

# Build GT table
GTTTtest2 = (
    GT(cooldata)
    .tab_header(title="Hypothesis Tests for UIP")
    .opt_stylize(5, "gray")
)

GTTTtest2
```

```{python}
#| layout-ncol: 1
#| label: monthly rolling
y = reg[main_y]
X = sm.add_constant(reg['Diff_lag']*100)

# Define the window size 
window_size = 6  # 1/2 year 

# Run the rolling regression
model = RollingOLS(y, X, window=window_size)
rolling_res = model.fit()
rolling_r2 = rolling_res.rsquared

# 4. Extract the coefficients (beta) and the 95% (1.96 is alpha/2) confidence intervals
params = rolling_res.params['Diff_lag']
std_err = rolling_res.bse['Diff_lag']

# 5. Plotting
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
plt.plot(df_monthly["Date"].drop(1), params, label='Rolling Beta (UIP Coefficient)', color='blue')
plt.axhline(y=1, color='red', linestyle='--', label='Theoretical UIP (Beta=1)')
plt.axhline(y=0, color='black', linewidth=0.8)
plt.title(f'Rolling UIP Regression ({window_size}-Period Window)')
plt.legend()

points = params.dropna().values
df_monthly["rollBETA"] = np.concat([[np.nan] *(window_size), points])
# 2. Use the "Pelt" algorithm to find breaks in the mean of the Beta
# 'model="l2"' looks for shifts in the average value of the beta
algo = rpt.Pelt(model="l2").fit(points)
result = algo.predict(pen=10) # 'pen' controls how 'strict' it is about finding a break

# You can then map these indices back to your dataframe dates.
break_dates = params.dropna().index[result[:-1]]

for i, b_date in enumerate(break_dates):
    plt.axvline(x=df_monthly[df_monthly.index == b_date]["Date"], color='darkorange', linestyle=':', lw=2, label='Structural Break' if i == 0 else "")
plt.grid(alpha=0.3)
plt.legend()
plt.show()

df_monthly[df_monthly.index.isin(break_dates)]
```

We see that using monthly data it holds better but never consistently at the $\beta = 1$ levels needed to confirm the existance of UIP in the dataset


```{python}
#| label: strat
#| layout-ncol: 1
#| html-table-processing: none

# Most carry trades use (Target_Rate - Funding_Rate)
df['Daily_Carry'] = (df['IR_US'] - df['IR_JP']) *12/252

# Total Daily Return = Interest Income + Spot Capital Gain
# Note: If USD/JPY (Spot) goes UP, the USD has appreciated = Profit
df['Strategy_Return'] = df['Daily_Carry'] + df[main_y]

# --- 2. Calculate Performance ---
# Calculate cumulative growth of $1
df['Cumulative_Carry_Return'] = (1 + df['Strategy_Return']).cumprod()
df['Cumulative_Spot_Only'] = (1 + df[main_y]).cumprod() # For comparison

# --- 3. Risk Metrics ---
ann_return = df['Strategy_Return'].mean() * 252
ann_vol = df['Strategy_Return'].std() * np.sqrt(252)
sharpe = ann_return / ann_vol

# Maximum Drawdown
rolling_max = df['Cumulative_Carry_Return'].cummax()
drawdown = (df['Cumulative_Carry_Return'] - rolling_max) / rolling_max
max_dd = drawdown.min()

# --- 4. Plotting ---
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
plt.plot(df['Date'], df['Cumulative_Carry_Return'], label='Carry Trade (Interest + Spot)', color='green', lw=2)
plt.plot(df['Date'], df['Cumulative_Spot_Only'], label='Spot Only', color='gray', alpha=0.5, linestyle='--')

plt.title(f'JPY/USD Carry Trade Performance ($\\beta=0$ Assumption)', fontsize=14)
plt.ylabel('Value of $1 Investment')
plt.legend()
plt.grid(alpha=0.3)
plt.show()

GTTTstrat1 = (
  GT(pd.DataFrame({"STATS" : ["Annualized Return", "Annualized Vol", "Sharpe Ratio", "Max Drawdown"], 
                    "VALS" : [ann_return, ann_vol, sharpe, max_dd]}))
  .fmt_percent(columns="VALS")
  .opt_stylize(5,"gray")
  .tab_header("Summary Statistics of Carry Trade Strategy")
)

GTTTstrat1
```
